title: Prosthechea hoehnei
taxonomy: Prosthechea hoehnei is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Prosthechea. It belongs to the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a subgroup of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Prosthechea hoehnei is further classified as part of the family Orchidaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. The species is also known to be part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which contains many species of orchids. Prosthechea hoehnei's classification is also known as an epiphytic orchid native to Central and South America.

intro: Prosthechea hoehnei, also known as the Hoehne's rat-tailed orchid, is a species of orchid found in the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Prosthechea hoehnei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Prosthechea hoehnei is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in various herbal blends to promote respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
